task_completion.description: Open the world_survey project and access the world_capitals layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GIS application (e.g., QField) is open, displaying the 'world_survey' project. The 'world_capitals' layer is visible in the layer panel or map.
The map or attribute table shows 'Bogota' at its correct location in Colombia (approx. 4.60, -74.08). The agent may just inspect it since it was not actually corrupted.
The map or attribute table shows 'Quito' at its correct location in Ecuador (approx. -0.22, -78.52). The agent may just inspect it since it was not actually corrupted.

task_completion.description: Create or edit the feature for Caracas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent opens an editing form or digitizing tool to create a new point feature and enters 'Caracas' into the name/attribute field.
The map shows the newly created 'Caracas' feature placed in Venezuela, northern South America (approx. 10.48, -66.90).

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS application GUI to verify and edit features.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a map interface (like QField), not just terminal SQLite commands.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely created the Caracas feature on the map.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visuals of the digitizing crosshairs, point placement, or attribute form being filled out for Caracas.

integrity.description: The agent did not just output a success message without performing the actions.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is actual visual proof of the map showing the three cities in their correct South American locations.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is 'world_survey.gpkg' and the layer is 'world_capitals'. Due to a setup bug, Bogota and Quito were never corrupted to Null Island (0,0) and remain at their correct coordinates (Bogota: 4.60, -74.08, Quito: -0.22, -78.52). The agent should leave them alone or verify their positions. Furthermore, 'Caracas' does not exist in the dataset at all. The agent must manually create a new point feature for Caracas at its real-world coordinates (approx 10.48, -66.90) using their own knowledge, as the 'description' field mentioned in the prompt does not exist.
